title: Late warnings and a minister’s fall: Latvia’s air-defence failures revealed
content: Latvia found itself at the centre of a national security storm last week. A series of drones crossed into its airspace, sparking not only a fire on the ground but also a political crisis that toppled the defence minister and exposed deep vulnerabilities in the Baltic state’s military readiness.

title: Europe Today: Trump meets Xi Jinping as Brussels watches nervously
content: All eyes are on Beijing, where Donald Trump has arrived alongside CEOs from some of the world’s biggest tech companies. On his first visit since 2017, the US president is meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping, with Brussels following nervously and fearing the worst.
snippet: All eyes are on Beijing, where Donald Trump has arrived alongside CEOs from some of the world’s biggest tech companies. On his first visit since 2017, the US president is meeting Chinese President Xi

title: Why the EU needs to rethink its budget to match its priorities
content: Experts warn that the EU’s next long-term budget needs “fundamental rethinking” and offers little real new spending power as Europe faces growing demands on defence, climate, and Ukraine.
snippet: Experts warn that the EU’s next long-term budget needs “fundamental rethinking” and offers little real new spending power as Europe faces growing demands on defence, climate, and Ukraine.

title: 'New People': Russia's Kremlin-made spoiler party is now its second most popular
content: New People, created by the Kremlin as a controlled outlet for dissent, has doubled its support to 13.4% as internet shutdowns fuel frustration with the Russian state.
snippet: New People, created by the Kremlin as a controlled outlet for dissent, has doubled its support to 13.4% as internet shutdowns fuel frustration with the Russian state.

title: Europe watches Beijing summit from the sidelines and fears the worst
content: The highly anticipated summit between US President Trump and his Chinese host Xi Jinping has begun - and Europe is watching from a distance. Yet, whatever the outcome is, there is little Brussels can be optimistic about.

title: Is Global Europe the answer to shifting alliances? MEPs debate in The Ring
content: In this edition of our weekly debate show The Ring, Damian Boeselager (Volt/Greens) and Helmut Brandstätter (Renew) discuss whether Europe can lead in a changing world order.
snippet: In this edition of our weekly debate show The Ring, Damian Boeselager (Volt/Greens) and Helmut Brandstätter (Renew) discuss whether Europe can lead in a changing world order.

title: Hungary's Magyar set to outline his red lines over EU funding talks in a letter to von der Leyen
content: Hungary's new PM Péter Magyar outlined how his government plans to spend EU funds, but said he will not phase out the controversial windfall taxes that Brussels has made a condition of their release.
snippet: Hungary's new PM Péter Magyar outlined how his government plans to spend EU funds, but said he will not phase out the controversial windfall taxes that Brussels has made a condition of their release.

title: Syria needs 'comprehensive security agreement' with Israel, foreign minister tells Euronews
content: Al-Shaibani calls for a return to the 1974 security agreement between Israel and Syria and demands the withdrawal from territories seized. Remarks coincide with high-level political dialogue between Damascus and Brussels.

title: EU Commission to dispatch team to Budapest as it mulls Hungarian investment bank for EU cash
content: The European Commission looks for options to disburse Hungarian cash, including through the country's investment bank, as it seeks to accelerate talks. A team of senior officials will be dispatched to Budapest next week.

title: EU to ban Brazilian meat imports from September
content: The ban would come just two weeks after the EU-Mercosur trade deal provisionally entered into force, liberalising agricultural trade across the Atlantic despite fierce opposition from farmers.
snippet: The ban would come just two weeks after the EU-Mercosur trade deal provisionally entered into force, liberalising agricultural trade across the Atlantic despite fierce opposition from farmers.

title: EU countries back suspension of funding for the Venice Biennale over Russian participation
content: Fourteen out of 27 EU member states criticised the reopening of the Russian pavilion at the Venice Biennale, which sparked a fierce controversy in Italy, as Brussels called for a suspension of funding.

title: General strike in Belgium against government reforms draws tens of thousands to the capital
content: The country’s three main trade unions have called for a nationwide protest against the government’s reforms.

title: Zelenskyy not implicated in a major graft probe, anti-corruption investigators say
content: “The President of Ukraine has neither been nor is currently a subject of the investigation,” the head of Ukraine’s anti-corruption bureau said on Tuesday, as the president’s former chief of staff appeared in court for a bail hearing.

title: Swedish crime kingpin linked to Foxtrot network arrested in Tunisia, police say
content: The Foxtrot criminal network was formed in the late 2010s and has been connected to dozens of violent crimes in Sweden.

title: EU's Kos sets July deadline for Ukraine accession clusters
content: The Enlargement Commissioner has set a July deadline to open all of Ukraine’s accession clusters, urging member states to speed up the process. Newly appointed Hungarian Prime Minister Magyar is set to play a key role.

title: 'US leadership has made NATO stronger than ever,' minister of North Macedonia tells Euronews
content: While many in Europe see Trump's policies as a threat to NATO, the Foreign Affairs Minister of North Macedonia told Euronews that the alliance has never been stronger than under President Trump's presidency.

title: Socialists rebuff EPP push to advance EU-US trade deal talks
content: EPP pushes to advance the next round of discussions to implement the EU-US trade deal, but Socialist chair Lange insists on keeping 19 May as the date for the next negotiating round, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: EU sanctions Russian individuals over forced deportation of Ukrainian children
content: The European Union imposed sanctions on 16 Russian individuals and seven entities for the systematic and ‌unlawful deportation of Ukrainian children, the EU Council said in a statement on Monday.
snippet: The European Union imposed sanctions on 16 Russian individuals and seven entities for the systematic and unlawful deportation of Ukrainian children, the EU Council said in a statement on Monday.

title: 'We need to make up our mind': EU still split over direct talks with Russia
content: The question of whether the EU should engage directly with Russia to end the war in Ukraine continues to split member states, as some prefer to bide their time and weaken Moscow's hand at the negotiating table.

title: Tariffs, weak demand and climate confusion drag EU business investment rate to 11-year low
content: It's the lowest point since 2015, with firms blaming geopolitical disruption, a disorderly market and regulation uncertainty. Hungary and Croatia buck the trend.
snippet: It's the lowest point since 2015, with firms blaming geopolitical disruption, a disorderly market and regulation uncertainty. Hungary and Croatia buck the trend.

title: Deepfake Meloni clips distort Italy and Israeli relations
content: Several viral deepfake clips have spread online with misleading claims about Italy’s relationship with Israel.
snippet: Several viral deepfake clips have spread online with misleading claims about Italy’s relationship with Israel.

title: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announces shock resignation
content: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announced on Thursday she will resign, triggering the collapse of the country's governing coalition ahead of key October parliamentary elections.
snippet: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announced on Thursday she will resign, triggering the collapse of the country's governing coalition ahead of key October parliamentary elections.

title: It will be hard to survive if the EU does not speak with one voice, MEP tells Euronews
content: The EU must realise that "it's difficult to survive" if it does not speak with one voice, Michael Bloss, a Greens MEP, tells Euronews. He made the comments off the back of US President Donald Trump visiting China to speak with President Xi Jinping about a raft of issues that could impact the bloc.

title: Zelenskyy's former chief of staff in custody amid major corruption investigation
content: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy remains silent on the case and the allegations against Andriy Yermak, who used to be the president's most trusted ally and Ukraine’s "number two."

title: Brazil ‘surprised’ by EU ban on meat imports
content: Brazil’s ambassador to the EU Pedro Miguel da Costa e Silva told Euronews he had asked the EU Commission to put Brazil back on the list of countries complying with EU antimicrobial rules. The move comes as the Mercosur deal liberalising agricultural trade came into force on 1 May.

title: Russia has launched over 800 drones against Ukraine since midnight, Zelenskyy says
content: Russia had mainly carried out mass drone and missile strikes at night, but the tactic changed in March, when Moscow switched to a daytime assault strategy.
snippet: Russia had mainly carried out mass drone and missile strikes at night, but the tactic changed in March, when Moscow switched to a daytime assault strategy.

title: Exclusive: EU negotiators find deal on key clauses of the EU-US deal
content: EU lawmakers and EU capitals struck a deal over provisions halting the EU-US deal if American imports surge unfairly. But crunch talks are still under way over when to start implementing the agreement, as negotiators face pressure from Trump’s threats of more tariffs.

title: EU to turn carbon tax revenues into farm subsidies amid fertiliser crisis, leaked document shows
content: Brussels is considering using revenues from the EU’s carbon market, the Emissions Trading System to cushion farmers from soaring energy and fertiliser prices, according to a document seen by Euronews.
snippet: Brussels is considering using revenues from the EU’s carbon market, the Emissions Trading System to cushion farmers from soaring energy and fertiliser prices, according to a document seen by Euronews.

title: 'Nothing to worry' about on hantavirus 'as long as we contain it', says expert
content: Professor Luke O'Neill, from Trinity College Dublin, acknowledges the virus situation is "a bit scary" given the mortality rate. But he asks authorities to "reassure" people about its low danger, as it is a known strain of hantavirus, the Andes variant, and it has "not changed".

title: ‘No question’ EU funds must not be misused, Estonian minister says
content: EU ministers met in Brussels this week to discuss how Ukraine could best use the EU’s €90 billion loan to repel Russian forces. Backdropped by a widening corruption scandal in the war-torn country, Estonia's defence minister told Euronews there is 'no question' the money should not be mishandled.

title: Germany plans 'active cyberdefence' as online attacks rise, interior ministry says
content: In 2025, around 334,000 cases of cybercrime were registered in Germany, two-thirds of them from abroad or unknown locations, with many more cases thought to be unreported.

title: Food prices could skyrocket, if no measures taken to tackle fertilizer crisis, farmer unions warn
content: The fertilizer crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is tripling costs for farmers. “Less fertilizers means less production for wheat. That’s the basis for bread,” warns COPA President, Massimiliano Giansanti.

title: European Commission faces backlash over plans to fast-track polluting projects

title: Farage surge makes Starmer's EU push 'more important than ever,' UK minister tells Euronews

title: EU could propose social media ban for children this summer, von der Leyen says
